title: quercus × howellii
intro: Quercus × howellii, also known as Howell's oak or Howell's white oak, is a rare, hybrid species of white oak native to the southwestern coast of California, USA. The medicinal properties of Quercus × howellii include its high content of flavonoids, tannins, and saponins, which exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable component in traditional herbal medicine. Quercus × howellii is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be administered orally or topically to harness its medicinal properties.
